topics_flat: Algebra > Algebraic Expressions > Sequences and Series > Telescoping series
Algebra > Algebraic Expressions > Sequences and Series > Sums and products
prompt:
Suppose that $f(x)$ is defined on $\mathbb{R}$, satisfying $f(0) = 2008$, and for any $x \in \mathbb{R}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
f(x+2) - f(x) &\le 3 \times 2^x, \\
f(x+6) - f(x) &\ge 63 \times 2^x.
\end{aligned}
$$

Then $f(2008) = \underline{\hspace{2cm}}$.
response_text:
Let
\[
a_n:=f(n)-2^n \qquad (n\in \mathbb Z).
\]
We will show \(a_n\) is constant on the even integers, which will determine \(f(2008)\).

From
\[
f(x+2)-f(x)\le 3\cdot 2^x,
\]
for integer \(x=n\),
\[
a_{n+2}-a_n
= \bigl(f(n+2)-2^{n+2}\bigr)-\bigl(f(n)-2^n\bigr)
= f(n+2)-f(n)-3\cdot 2^n
\le 0.
\]
So
\[
a_{n+2}\le a_n. \tag{1}
\]

From
\[
f(x+6)-f(x)\ge 63\cdot 2^x,
\]
again for integer \(x=n\),
\[
a_{n+6}-a_n
= f(n+6)-f(n)-(2^{n+6}-2^n)
\ge 63\cdot 2^n-63\cdot 2^n=0.
\]
Hence
\[
a_{n+6}\ge a_n. \tag{2}
\]

Now apply (1) successively:
\[
a_{n+6}\le a_{n+4}\le a_{n+2}\le a_n.
\]
Together with (2), this gives
\[
a_{n+6}=a_{n+4}=a_{n+2}=a_n.
\]
So \(a_n\) is periodic with period \(2\) on integers; in particular, all even-indexed terms are equal.

Since \(2008\) is even,
\[
a_{2008}=a_0=f(0)-2^0=2008-1=2007.
\]
Therefore
\[
f(2008)=a_{2008}+2^{2008}=2007+2^{2008}.
\]

Final answer: \(2^{2008}+2007\)
